title: Box Office Friday Results: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple Takes Top Spot
text: Nia DaCosta's horror sequel 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple claimed the number one position on Friday, earning $5.6 million across 3,506 North American theaters. The film is projected to reach approximately $15 million through the four-day Martin Luther King Jr. holiday weekend. This fourth installment in Sony's zombie franchise features Ralph Fiennes, Jack O'Connell, and Alfie Williams. The screenplay was written by Alex Garland, and the production carried a budget of $63 million. Critics responded positively, and audiences awarded it an A- CinemaScore rating, described as rare within the horror genre. The previous box office leader, Avatar: Fire and Ash, dropped to second place with $3.2 million on its fifth Friday. The James Cameron film is anticipated to accumulate roughly $17 million over the holiday frame, bringing its domestic total to approximately $367 million by Monday. The original 28 Years Later opened with $30 million over three days last summer and ultimately earned $150 million globally. This sequel's stronger production budget reflects increased investment in the franchise.
